title: Turing Machine Interactive Lab
subject: Theory of Computation
description: Complete interactive guide to Turing Machines. Explore the tape model, 7-tuple definition, and a library of 9 functional machines from simple scanners to Busy Beavers.
learnPoints: Master the formal 7-tuple definition of TMs
Visualize tape-head interactions and execution traces
Explore 9 curated machines from binary adders to Busy Beavers
Understand the Church-Turing Thesis and Halting Problem

title: File Allocation & I/O Buffering
subject: Operating Systems
description: Interactive visualization of continuous, linked, and indexed file allocation methods alongside I/O buffering strategies.
learnPoints: Compare contiguous, linked, and indexed allocation
Understand internal and external fragmentation
Analyze I/O buffering impact on performance

title: Memory Partitioning
subject: Operating Systems
description: Simulate and compare First Fit, Best Fit, and Worst Fit memory allocation strategies with dynamic memory blocks.
learnPoints: Visualize First Fit, Best Fit, and Worst Fit algorithms
Understand dynamic memory partitioning logic
Observe the effects of memory fragmentation

title: Sorting Algorithms Visualizer
subject: Data Structures
description: Visualize Bubble, Selection, Insertion, Quick, Merge and Heap sort with step-by-step animations and complexity analysis.
learnPoints: Understand core sorting logic for 6 major algorithms
Analyze time and space complexity visually
Differentiate between stable and unstable sorts
Learn when to use which sorting algorithm

title: Search Visualizer
subject: Data Structures
description: Visualize Linear Search and Binary Search algorithms with live comparisons and step tracking.
learnPoints: Master the divide and conquer approach of Binary Search
Visualize the sequential nature of Linear Search
Compare search efficiencies on sorted vs unsorted data
Understand O(log n) vs O(n) performance

title: Stack Visualizer
subject: Data Structures
description: Interactive stack operations: push, pop, peek with array and linked representation.
learnPoints: Understand LIFO (Last In First Out) principle
Visualize push and pop operations in real-time
Learn about stack overflow and underflow conditions
Compare array vs linked list stack implementations

title: Queue Visualizer
subject: Data Structures
description: Visualize Queue (Array Implementation) with enqueue, dequeue and front/rear tracking.
learnPoints: Understand FIFO (First In First Out) principle
Visualize front and rear pointer movements
Master enqueue and dequeue operations
Learn about the limitations of simple queues

title: Circular Queue Visualization
subject: Data Structures
description: Visualize circular queue operations with wrap-around pointer logic.
learnPoints: Master the modulo arithmetic for wrap-around indices
Visualize efficient space utilization in circular buffers
Understand the 'full' vs 'empty' state ambiguity
Identify real-world use cases for circular queues

title: Expression Conversion Visualizer
subject: Data Structures
description: Convert Infix to Postfix and Prefix using stack. Watch each step of the conversion visually.
learnPoints: Learn stack applications in expression evaluation
Visualize operator precedence and associativity logic
Master the algorithm for infix to postfix conversion
Understand how compilers parse arithmetic expressions

title: CPU Scheduling Simulator
subject: Operating Systems
description: Simulate FCFS, SJF, Priority and Round Robin scheduling. View Gantt charts, waiting time and turnaround time.
learnPoints: Compare preemptive vs non-preemptive scheduling
Understand Gantt chart construction
Calculate average waiting and turnaround times
Analyze the 'convoy effect' and starvation issues

title: Disk Scheduling Visualizer
subject: Operating Systems
description: Trace FCFS, SSTF, SCAN, C-SCAN and LOOK disk arm movement. Compare total seek time across algorithms.
learnPoints: Visualize disk arm movement patterns
Understand seek time optimization
Compare directional algorithms like SCAN and LOOK
Analyze fairness vs efficiency trade-offs

title: Page Replacement Simulator
subject: Operating Systems
description: Simulate FIFO, LRU and Optimal page replacement algorithms. Track page faults in real time.
learnPoints: Understand virtual memory and demand paging
Visualize page fault handling
Compare hit ratios across different algorithms
Learn about Belady's Anomaly in FIFO

title: Line Algorithm Comparator
subject: Computer Graphics
description: Compare DDA and Bresenham's line drawing algorithms pixel-by-pixel on a zoomed grid. Step through each decision, inspect the Bresenham pₖ parameter live, and watch both algorithms draw side by side.
learnPoints: Understand how DDA uses floating-point parametric increments
Master Bresenham's integer decision parameter pₖ and its update rule
Compare pixel choices between DDA and Bresenham for the same line
Recognize why Bresenham is preferred in hardware rasterization

title: ER Diagram Builder
subject: Database Management System
description: Drag and drop entities, define relationships and attributes. Generate ER diagrams visually.
learnPoints: Master entity-relationship modeling concepts
Visualize key constraints and cardinalities
Learn to design normalized database schemas
Differentiate between weak and strong entities
